$comment: Editable tool descriptions and instructions. Tool names and JSON parameter schemas stay in code.

get_time.description: Return local date, time, timezone and uptime.
get_time.instructions: Use this when the user asks about the current time, date, timezone, or how long the robot has been running.

robot_status.description: Return basic runtime, platform and robot state.
robot_status.instructions: Use this when the user asks about battery, status, health, platform, active model, or robot state.

set_eye_expression.description: Set the robot eye expression state.
set_eye_expression.instructions: Use this for visible emotion or attention state changes. Prefer thinking while working, happy/excited for positive reactions, confused/worried when uncertain.

calculator.description: Evaluate a safe math expression with functions like sqrt, sin, gcd, lcm, round, floor and ceil.
calculator.instructions: Use this for arithmetic, roots, trigonometry, rounding, gcd/lcm, powers, and similar exact math. Do not do mental math when this tool can answer precisely.

remember.description: Persist a short user-provided memory for future chats.
remember.instructions: Use this when the user says phrases like "remember this", "remember that", or asks you to remember a preference, fact, name, project detail, or instruction for later.

take_photo.description: Capture a photo if a configured safe camera command exists.
take_photo.instructions: Use this only when a camera command is configured and the user asks to see, look, inspect, or take a photo.

run_safe_cli_tool.description: Run one whitelisted local command by name.
run_safe_cli_tool.instructions: Use this only for commands explicitly listed in configuration. Never request raw shell access.
